Name: McLaren
1Q: Where is McLaren Technology Center located?
tip1: McLaren Group is consists of McLaren Racing, McLaren Automotive, and McLaren Applied Technologies.
2Q: Which animal was an integral part of McLaren's first logo?
tip2: McLaren founder Bruce Mclaren was from New Zeland, where Kiwi is a national animal.
3Q: What abbreviation in the name does all the McLarens Formula 1 cars contain from 1981.?
tip3: MP4 first stands for Marlboro Project 4, but now stands for McLaren Project 4.
4Q: Whose V12 engine uses model F1, world's fastest naturally aspirated production car ever?
tip4: F1 concept is conceived by legendary engineer and designer Gordon Murray.
5Q: With whom McLaren developed the SLR model?
tip5: There is a dedicated version of SLR made in honor of racing legend Sir Stirling Moss.
6Q: Which is the first-ever production car wholly designed and built by McLaren Group?
tip6: Launching of model 12C was in 2011, and it represents a new era for McLaren Automotive.
7Q: What engine configuration use hybrid Mclaren P1?
tip7: V8 engine used from 12C to P1 was originally developed for Nissan racing cars back in the 80s for Le Mans endurance races.
8Q: What is the name of Mclaren's bespoke division, officially launched in 2011?
tip8: MSO provides everything from bespoke customization options through to building ultra-exclusive limited-edition or one-off models.
9Q: In honor of which Formula One driver McLaren introduced limited-production race car in 2017?
tip9: McLaren Senna is the third addition in the McLaren Ultimate Series of vehicles.
10Q: What is the primary purpose of Mclaren Speedtail?
tip10: Design of McLaren Speedtail is subordinate to achieving maximum speed.
